title: Cross-Attention
summary: Q from one source, K/V from another. Enables connecting different data types/modalities. Score matrix is rectangular [n_tgt × n_src]. K and V always come from the same source. No mask applied.
key_insights: Self-attention: Q=K=V source. Cross-attention: Q from one source, K/V from another
K and V always from SAME source (keys advertise, values deliver)
Score matrix RECTANGULAR [n_tgt × n_src] — not square
Swapping Q and K/V sources is NOT symmetric
Pattern applies beyond language: RAG, knowledge graphs, sensor analysis

mathematical_core.formula: CrossAttention(Q_dec, K_enc, V_enc) = softmax(Q_dec · K_encᵀ / √d_k) · V_enc
mathematical_core.output: [B, n_tgt, d] — target's sequence length, not source's

derivations.title: Asymmetry of Q vs K/V assignment

steps.name: Configuration A: Q=decoder, K/V=encoder
steps.math: Score S[i,j] = Q_dec[i] · K_enc[j]ᵀ / √d_k. Output[i] = Σⱼ softmax(S[i,:])_j · V_enc[j]. Each decoder position retrieves a weighted blend of encoder content.

steps.name: Configuration B: Q=encoder, K/V=decoder (swapped)
steps.math: Score S[i,j] = Q_enc[i] · K_dec[j]ᵀ / √d_k. Output[i] = Σⱼ softmax(S[i,:])_j · V_dec[j]. Each encoder position retrieves a weighted blend of decoder content.

steps.name: Key difference
steps.math: Config A output shape: [B, n_tgt, d] — one vector per decoder position. Config B output shape: [B, n_src, d] — one vector per encoder position. The OUTPUT inherits the Q source's sequence length.

derivations.consequence: The Q source determines 'who is asking questions' and gets the output. The K/V source determines 'what knowledge is available'. In encoder-decoder: the decoder asks, the encoder provides. Swapping would mean the encoder is querying the decoder — semantically different.

derivations.title: Rectangular attention matrix properties

steps.name: Shape
steps.math: A ∈ [n_tgt, n_src]. Each row sums to 1 (softmax over source positions). But columns do NOT sum to 1.

steps.name: Column interpretation
steps.math: Column j of A tells you how much each target position attends to source position j. If column j sums to a large value, source position j is heavily attended (high 'coverage'). If column j sums near 0, source position j is ignored.

steps.name: Coverage diagnostic
steps.math: Source coverage = Σᵢ A[i,j] for each source position j. In translation: every source word should be 'covered' (attended to by at least one target word). Low coverage = missed source content.

derivations.consequence: Coverage analysis is a diagnostic tool: if the cross-attention consistently ignores certain encoder positions, the model is missing input information. This was a known issue in early seq2seq models, partly solved by coverage mechanisms.

derivations.title: Computational complexity comparison
derivations.math: Self-attention: O(n² · d_k) — quadratic in ONE sequence length. Cross-attention: O(n_tgt · n_src · d_k) — product of TWO lengths. If n_tgt << n_src (short query, long knowledge base): dominated by n_src. If n_tgt ≈ n_src: similar to self-attention.
derivations.consequence: For applications with long knowledge bases (large n_src) and short queries (small n_tgt), cross-attention is dominated by the source length. This is why efficient retrieval (reducing n_src before cross-attention) matters.

geometric_properties: Cross-attention creates an alignment manifold between two representation spaces
For translation: expect roughly diagonal pattern (with deviations for word-order changes)
Monitor: does decoder attend to few encoder positions (focused retrieval) or broadly (aggregation)?
statistical_properties: Source coverage: Σᵢ A[i,j] per source position — should be non-zero for all informative positions
Attention diversity across heads: different heads should align different aspects
Cross-attention entropy: peaked = retrieval task, broad = aggregation task
numerical_properties: O(n_tgt · n_src · d_k) per head — not O(n²) unless sequences are equal length
Memory for attention weights: B × h × n_tgt × n_src
If n_src >> n_tgt: memory dominated by source length
application_ideas: RAG: user query → Q, retrieved documents → K/V
Knowledge graph: query → Q, graph node embeddings → K/V
Hypothesis discovery: self-attention discovers patterns, cross-attention matches against references
notes: Strong intuition developed through application reasoning rather than formal study
